title: Bus message authentication (HMAC)

vulnerability_analysis.description: The file-based message bus (/tmp/manusat-bus/) currently operates without authentication or message integrity verification. Any process with filesystem access can forge, tamper, or replay messages.

attack_vectors.name: Message Forgery
attack_vectors.description: Attacker can create messages with forged 'from' field, impersonating any agent (e.g., jit, soma, innova)
attack_vectors.impact: HIGH - Can execute arbitrary commands by spoofing master agents
attack_vectors.example: Create task: message claiming to be from 'jit' ordering deploy to production

attack_vectors.name: Message Tampering
attack_vectors.description: Attacker can modify message content during transit before agent reads it
attack_vectors.impact: HIGH - Can alter task parameters, inject malicious code in 'body'

attack_vectors.name: Message Replay
attack_vectors.description: Attacker can replay old messages multiple times
attack_vectors.impact: MEDIUM - Can trigger duplicate executions, but requires idempotency-key dedup
attack_vectors.example: Re-send old 'task:deploy' from months ago to trigger unexpected deployment

attack_vectors.name: DLQ Injection
attack_vectors.description: Attacker can inject fake failures or move legitimate messages to DLQ
attack_vectors.impact: MEDIUM - Can cause denial of service for specific agents
attack_vectors.example: Overwrite .reason sidecars to confuse retry logic

current_state.replay_protection: PARTIAL - Idempotency keys exist but not enforced uniformly
current_state.secret_management: NONE - No shared secret distribution mechanism

vulnerability_analysis.root_cause: Protocol v1.0 added headers (protocol-version, idempotency-key, x-signature field) but signature computation and verification were not fully implemented in mouth.sh (send) and ear.sh (receive).
affected_components: network/bus.sh - No signature generation when sending
organs/mouth.sh - Does not compute or add x-signature header
organs/ear.sh - Does not verify x-signature before processing
limbs/lib.sh - Has functions defined but not integrated
network/protocol.md - Spec exists but implementation incomplete

fix_design.approach: End-to-end HMAC-SHA256 signature signing and verification
design_principles: Sign at send time (mouth.sh) over canonical string: from+to+subject+timestamp+body
Verify at receive time (ear.sh) before processing
Strict mode: MANUSAT_STRICT_AUTH=1 (require signatures) or 0 (legacy compat)
Backward compatible: Accept unsigned messages when STRICT_AUTH=0
Constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks

1_send.step: Prepare message: from, to, subject, timestamp, body

3_add_header.step: Add x-signature header to message file

2_parse_headers.step: Extract from, to, subject, timestamp, x-signature, body

3_verify.step: Recompute HMAC-SHA256 and compare with provided signature

conditions: Invalid signature → Move to rejected/ directory, log EAR_REJECTED
Missing signature + STRICT_AUTH=1 → Reject with error
Missing signature + STRICT_AUTH=0 → Accept with warning

changes: Update message format example to show x-signature header
Add signature verification as mandatory step in lifecycle
Document canonical string format and HMAC algorithm
Add JIT-011 reference in version history
Update error handling section with signature failures
ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: bus_compute_signature() - compute HMAC-SHA256
ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: bus_verify_signature() - verify signature
ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: bus_parse_signature() - extract from headers
ADD: Constant-time comparison safeguard (optional enhancement)
ADD: Logging for signature operations (already has err/warn)
In 'send' command: Call bus_compute_signature() after building message
Add x-signature header to message before writing to file
Log signature generation with ok/info
Handle case when MANUSAT_BUS_SECRET not set (warn but allow)
In tell() function: Call bus.sh signature functions
Integrate signature generation before message write
Add x-signature header to outgoing messages
Log signature generation attempt
Add _verify_message() helper function
Call _verify_message() in receive() and listen() before processing
Create rejected/ directory for failed signature verification
Move invalid-signature messages to rejected/ with .reason sidecar
Log EAR_REJECTED action for audit trail
Dedicated authentication utility (optional, for debugging/testing)
Commands: sign <msg-file>, verify <msg-file>, test-round-trip
Useful for manual testing and troubleshooting auth issues

tests_required.name: Valid signature acceptance
tests_required.description: Message with valid HMAC-SHA256 signature is accepted and processed
steps: 1. Create message: from=innova, to=soma, subject=task:test, timestamp=2026-06-08T10:00:00, body=test
tests_required.expected: Message processed, logged BUS_RECV, NOT in rejected/

tests_required.name: Invalid signature rejection
tests_required.description: Message with tampered body is rejected (signature mismatch)
steps: 1. Create message with valid signature for body='original'
tests_required.expected: Message moved to rejected/, logged EAR_REJECTED, NOT processed

tests_required.name: Missing signature in strict mode rejection
tests_required.expected: Message moved to rejected/, logged BUS_AUTH_FAIL error

tests_required.name: Missing signature legacy mode acceptance
tests_required.expected: Message processed with warning, logged BUS_AUTH legacy message

tests_required.name: No secret configured behavior
steps: 1. Send message via bus.sh send to innova subject test body hello
2. Check message file for x-signature header
tests_required.expected: Message sent without x-signature header, warning logged

tests_required.name: Signature format correctness
tests_required.description: Canonical string includes all required fields (from+to+subject+timestamp+body)
steps: 1. Manually compute: openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac 'test-secret' with exact canonical

success_criteria: Signature header present in all new messages (when secret configured)
